title: Using Zapier with API v2 - update column value with DATE
excerpt:
Hi 
I’m using Zapier to take info from a Keap Pro Internal Smart Form, to set up a series of events in Zapier. 
I am using API v2.

posts.author: ThoughtPenny (Fiona Campbell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi

I’m using Zapier to take info from a Keap Pro Internal Smart Form, to set up a series of events in Zapier.

I am using API v2.

I can add new items to boards, create an update, and update most columns for the item created without any issues.

The only one I am struggling with is the date column.

I can see these instructions:

  • Date Column with No Time - {“date”:“2020-12-03”}
  • Date Column with Time - {“date”:“2020-12-03”,“time”:“13:25:00”}

But the date format I get from the form completed in Keap Pro doesn’t give me a date in the required format above!

This is what I tried, which failed:

Screen Shot 2020-06-18 at 11.06.49 am917×594 33.6 KB

Any ideas on how this can be automated… or is there no way to reconcile the date format Keap Pro gives me with what monday.com needs? The Keap Pro date format does work setting a Zoom meeting date, so I am hoping there is a trick to getting it to work with monday.com!

posts.author: mannymonday (Manny)
posts.cookedHtml:
You’ll need to add a step in Zapier to correct the format.
This should help.

Zapier

Modify date and time formats in Zaps

Formatter: Dates & Times You can use Formatter’s Date/Time transform to change dates and times in your Zap. The Date/Time transform can reformat dates and times or add/subtract time from exis...

I have a problem with getting the format out. I followed the instructions you refer to, but they didn’t work.

posts.author: ThoughtPenny (Fiona Campbell)
posts.cookedHtml: LEGEND @mannymonday - worked perfectly. Thank you

posts.author: TMNXT-Dev (Ilja Zweigert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mannymonday,

thanks for helping out. Really usefull resource

Greetings

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mannymonday LEGENDARY! thank you for the assist! Glad this is still helping out the community

-Daniel
